title: Efficient Optimization of Variational Quantum Algorithms via Gradient-Free Parameter Prediction
authors: Satwik Kundu, Debarshi Kundu, Swaroop Ghosh
abstract: The exponential runtime of quantum simulators on classical machines, along with long queue times and high costs associated with real quantum devices, presents significant challenges for the efficient optimization of Variational Quantum Algorithms (VQAs) such as the Variational Quantum Eigensolver (VQE), Quantum Approximate Optimization Algorithm (QAOA), and Quantum Neural Networks (QNNs). To address these limitations, we propose a novel approach, DyPP (Dynamic Parameter Prediction), which accelerates the convergence of VQAs by leveraging regular trends in the parameter weights to update parameters efficiently. We introduce two techniques for optimal prediction performance: Naive Prediction (NaP) and Adaptive Prediction (AdaP). Through extensive experimentation and training of multiple QNN models on various datasets, we demonstrate that DyPP achieves a speedup of approximately 2.25×compared to standard training methods, while also providing improved accuracy (up to 2.3% higher) and lower loss (up to 6.1% reduction) with minimal storage and computational overhead. We further evaluate DyPP’s effectiveness in VQE for molecular ground-state energy estimation and in QAOA for graph MaxCut. Our results show that, on average, DyPP leads to a speedup of up to 3.1× for VQE and 2.91× for QAOA compared to traditional optimization, while requiring up to 3.3× fewer shots (i.e., repeated circuit executions). Even in the presence of hardware noise, DyPP outperforms existing optimization techniques, delivering up to 3.33× speedup and requiring 2.5× fewer shots, thereby enhancing the efficiency of VQAs.

title: BANet: Enhancing Weakly Aligned Multimodal Object Detection via Balanced Bidirectional Alignment Network
authors: Yutian Shi, Guoquan Li, Zhilong Shen, Hongying Meng, Yu Pang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Multimodal object detection in remote sensing imagery has achieved remarkable performance, primarily owing to its ability to exploit complementary information from multiple modalities. However, most existing methods often suffer from substantial performance degradation under weakly aligned conditions, primarily due to the asymmetric utilization of information across different modalities. Therefore, we propose a novel multi-modal object detection network, termed Bidirectional Alignment Network (BANet), which aims to improve detection accuracy in weakly aligned multimodal remote sensing imagery by adopting a dual-path architecture and incorporating a dedicated Weakly Aligned Module (WAM) to explicitly mitigate misalignment and enhance cross-modal feature interaction. Specifically, WAM includes three cooperative components. Firstly, the Adaptive Cross-Modal Correlation Module (ACMCM) is designed to establish semantic correspondence by jointly modeling global dependencies and local similarities in a bidirectional manner. Then, the Symmetric Offset Generator (SOG) adopts a coarse-to-fine strategy to produce stable and symmetric offsets, thereby enabling precise and robust spatial alignment. Finally, the Progressive Fusion Strategy (PFS) adaptively integrates the original and aligned features through learnable weighting, effectively preserving modality-specific characteristics while enhancing both spatial alignment and semantic consistency. Extensive experiments on the DroneVehicle and VEDAI multimodal remote sensing datasets demonstrate the superiority of the proposed method over other advanced multimodal remote sensing object detectors. Notably, BANet performs best on the two datasets with only 8.8M parameters, highlighting its effectiveness and efficiency for real-time UAV applications.

title: Incorporating contextual cross-attention into encoding-decoding architecture for single-image dehazing
authors: He Deng, Yuqing Li, Kai Cheng, Dan Zhu
abstract: In deep learning-based dehazing strategies, attention mechanisms are widely used to refine feature representations and improve overall performance. However, conventional contextual attention mechanisms often extract overly generalized or isolated contextual information, failing to capture the intricate multi-scale and hierarchical dependencies necessary for distinguishing subtle differences between reference and target images. To overcome this limitation, we introduce the contextual cross-attention embedded network (C2A-Net), specifically designed to better address fine-grained variations and spatially inconsistent haze distributions. Drawing inspiration from the significance of priors in enhancing dehazing performance, C2A-Net incorporates priors derived from a fixed, pre-trained dehazing model within an encoding-decoding architecture. The encoder employs multi-scale feature extractors to aggregate information across levels, while the decoder leverages contextual cross-attention to enhance spatial and channel context, effectively handling non-uniform haze distributions. Moreover, to mitigate impact of undefined degradations, consistency constraint is introduced to minimize both global perceptual and local spatial discrepancies between predictions and ground truths, ensuring the reliability of the dehazing process. Experimental results validate that C2A-Net obtains superior robustness and effectiveness across diverse hazy conditions, outperforming state-of-the-art methods in both qualitative and quantitative evaluations.

title: Data-Driven Precision Velocity Control for Maglev Car Systems via Error-Scheduled Model-Free Adaptive Control
authors: Yiying Yang, Zhihao Ke, Jingguo Bi, Zhengyan Li, Jun Zheng, Zigang Deng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Driven by the growing demand for clean and high-velocity transportation, maglev car systems have emerged as promising solutions. Precise velocity control is crucial for the safe and stable operation of maglev cars. However, strong nonlinearities and underdamped dynamics of these systems make accurate modeling difficult, which limits the performance of traditional model-based controllers. Therefore, research in this area remains limited. While Model-Free Adaptive Control (MFAC) offers a potential solution, standard algorithms with fixed parameters that struggle to balance response velocity and stability. To overcome these limitations, this paper proposes an MFAC framework integrated with error gain scheduling (EGS). The EGS dynamically adjusts the controller’s parameters based on real-time tracking error regions, thereby mitigating the trade-off between transient response rate and overshoot. The MFAC‑EGS scheme preserves low computational complexity and real‑time adaptability, requiring neither a precise model nor extensive offline training. The primary contributions of this work include: (1) A velocity control scheme for maglev cars is developed to ensure stable velocity regulation under complex conditions, which is essential for operational safety and stability; (2) A data-driven MFAC-EGS paradigm is established, which enhances MFAC robustness against disturbances without increasing structural complexity; (3) Experimental results demonstrate that the maglev car velocity control function is achieved under various controllers, among which MFAC-EGS shows the superior control performance. Specifically, the proposed method reduces the root mean square error (RMSE) by 20.9% in steady-state tracking and 22.9% under disturbed conditions. It also achieves reductions of 50.5% and 21.5% for triangular and square-wave signals, respectively, alongside faster response and lower overshoot. This work offers valuable insights into the future high-speed operation of maglev cars.

title: Transfer Learning-Based Deep Reinforcement Learning for Adaptive Control of Maglev Trains
authors: Su-Mei Wang, Qi Zhu, Yi-Qing Ni
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: As a key component of a maglev train, the magnetic suspension control system ensures that the airgap between the train and its guideway is stable. Although current suspension controllers meet basic engineering requirements, problems related to their performance often arise during long-term operations. These problems can seriously affect the stability and reliability of a maglev system and may lead to partial suspension-point failure. Hence, this paper uses transfer learning-based deep reinforcement learning to develop an adaptive nonlinear suspension system controller that enables automatic adjustment of control strategies. First, suspension control based on deep reinforcement learning is mathematically modeled using Markov decision processes, and the nonlinear state space of a single electromagnet suspension control system is established as an agent–environment interaction with the developed deep reinforcement learning controller. Then a twin delayed deep deterministic policy gradient algorithm in an actor–critic framework is adopted to solve the Markov decision processes. To address the dispersion caused by nonlinear suspension control, a transfer learning-based two-stage training process is devised that first trains the twin delayed deep deterministic policy gradient networks on a linearized model and then transfers the networks to a nonlinear model. The effectiveness of the new controller is verified by comparing it with a conventional proportional–integral–derivative controller and a neural network based adaptive super-twisting sliding mode controller. The robustness of the transfer learning–deep reinforcement learning controller is examined in the presence of uncertainty, such as train load changes and disturbance forces in the suspension system. Additionally, experiments have also been conducted to confirm the effectiveness of the TL–DRL controller on a magnetic levitation system.

title: Do-It-Yourself Sheet-Metal Open TEM Cell
authors: Giacomo Giannetti, Norbert Leder, Thomas Weiler, Christian Spindelberger, Holger Arthaber
publish: IEEE Letters on Electromagnetic Compatibility Practice and Applications
abstract: This paper presents an improved open transverse electromagnetic (TEM) cell for pre-compliance testing. The design shows a 300 mm septum height and works over CISPR Bands C and D (from 30 MHz to 1 GHz). The improvement lies in its do-it-yourself construction, enabling in-house manufacturing using laser-cut, bendable sheet metals with rectangular slots removed to ease bending. The resulting structure is cost-effective and mechanically robust. 3D-printed supports extending only between the septum and the upper conductor improve the accessibility to the test volume. The design is tested experimentally (minimum return loss of 11.5 dB), and its reproducibility is validated over four independently assembled samples.

title: Automated Multilingual Narrative Analytics Using BERTopic: Transforming Student Feedback into Actionable Educational Insights
authors: Said Almaqbali, Asmaa H. Marhoubi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: This work presents an automation approach that is capable of automatically reading and comprehending feedback of Arabic-English students to assist schools in becoming better. We reviewed 431 free-text responses to seven survey questions. In the proposed work, words were analyzed through a process known as language analysis and the topic-finding algorithm, named BERTopic, to sort the comments and extract key ideas. The difference between question to question was very big: Question 5 (things students like) had the largest number of positive comments (149), and Question 6 (things students dislike) had the largest number of negative comments (174). In case of instructor questions (Q1), the answers were combined, 83 negative and 123 positive. The largest question (Course questions (Q2) reflected concerns about the curriculum most, and 154 negative comments were made. Some of the major themes that the topic modeling identified include good teaching environments, library and extracurricular activities requirements, and exam stress. It had an automatic recommendation for each theme. This framework demonstrates an easy-to-follow, scalable format for transforming the stories by multilingual students into practical insights to enable schools to make better-informed and data-driven decisions.

title: Dynamic Point Cloud Completion for Neonatal Respiratory Movement Monitoring Based on L-PF-Net Network
authors: Liangyu Zhang, Xueqing Yu, Shumei Yu, Rongchuan Sun, Hongqing Guo, Wenying Yao, Lining Sun
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Micro/Nano Sensors for AI, Healthcare, and Robotics (NSENS)
abstract: Neonatal respiratory monitoring requires a system capable of capturing chest and abdominal movements accurately and in real time. However, missing data can misjudge respiratory frequency, amplitude, and patterns, affecting the accuracy of monitoring. In order to mitigate the problem of misdiagnoses resulting from incomplete data in neonatal respiratory surveillance, this paper proposes a multi-feature fusion point cloud completion network named L-PF-Net that integrates PF-Net with Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM). This network significantly improves the prediction capability of continuous frame changes in dynamic point clouds, improving monitoring accuracy and clinical application value. Experimental results show that L-PF-Net reduces the average Chamfer Distance (CD) error by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2. 0 3 4 4 m m}$</tex> compared to PF-Net, with Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) and Mean Absolute Error (MAE) values of 2.2108 mm and 1.80493 mm, respectively. This provides reliable data support for more accurately capturing neonatal respiratory motion features and early detection of respiratory anomalies.

title: SECCOMMS: AI-Driven Visual Steganography for Secure Communication
authors: Abdulla Ahmad J. A. Al-Hail, Jihad Mohamad Alja’am
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: This paper presents SecComms, a unified secure- communication application that combines a PGP-style hybrid cryptosystem (RSA for key encapsulation and AES for payload confidentiality) with image steganography over AI-generated covers produced via the DALL•E API. SecComms provides an end-to-end workflow—including key management, hybrid encryption, image generation, least-significant-bit (LSB)-style embedding, GUI-driven user interaction, authentication via a lightweight Flask server, and error handling—implemented in Python with a desktop GUI. The system’s objective is to reduce user burden while adding a covert channel such that both the content and the existence of the message are protected. Functional tests on 1920×1080 PNG covers show reliable embedding/extraction within practical payload sizes, acceptable runtime latency for interactive use, and positive usability scores from non-expert participants. Security trade-offs, limitations of LSB against advanced steganalysis, and future hardening paths (e.g., adaptive embedding, robust watermarking, and postquantum cryptography) are discussed, positioning SecComms as a practical baseline for research on covert, user-friendly secure messaging.

title: FW-UAV Fault Diagnosis Method Based on Transfer Learning
authors: Deyong Xiao, Yizong Zhang, Jie Fang, Yingzhou Li, Liya Yu, Shaobo Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: The widespread use of deep learning has led to more advanced solutions for unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) fault diagnosis(FD). However, most existing deep learning methods are designed for single-case tasks. In real life, the operating environment of UAVs is complex and varied. This makes it difficult for these methods to adapt to multiple realworld, variable-case tasks. Additionally, UAV fault samples are scarce. Thus, we propose a transfer learning method with high generalization ability: MMRFD. First, MMRFD disperses the features extracted by the feature extractor by sparsifying the data in multiple layers. This ensures that the model learns more generalized features and solves the problem of domain bias by minimizing the distance between different domains through maximal mean difference. The efficacy of proposed methodology is subsequently validated by means of FW-UAV flight data.

title: An Automated Personal Protective Equipment Detection System Using Deep Learning
authors: Aishath Nahufa
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Innovations in Engineering, Science and Technology for Sustainable Development (ICEST)
abstract: This paper presents the development and implementation of an Automated Safety Gear Detection System designed to improve workplace safety through real-time computer vision monitoring. The system utilizes You Only Look Once deep learning model to automatically detect whether workers are wearing the necessary safety gear such as helmets, vests and shoes before they enter risky environments. The developed system addresses safety challenges in the current inconsistent manual monitoring prone to human error by providing automatic monitoring and issuing alerts to workers. The system detects pre-defined safety gears and the absence or presence of them. The workers are required to align to the video frame for proper detection and achieves accurate detections under optimal lighting. The system is built on Flask-based web architecture, integrating OpenCV for video processing, PyTorch for model inference and SQLite for database management. The methodology followed five key phases: dataset collection, data processing, model training, system development and testing with validation. Key functions of the system include bilingual (English and Dhivehi) notifications and sound alerts for compliance and violations. The implementation of a user-friendly web interface allows users to monitor detections in real-time and detections history analyzing. Performance evaluation indicates reliable detection accuracy in real-world settings. The automatic PPE detection system offers a reliable and cost-effective solution for the current inconsistent monitoring by ensuring continuous PPE compliance.

title: Smart University Student Quality Development Management App Design and Implementation
authors: Xinyu Lv, Qiuzhen Yan, Siyuan Li, Weili Qiu, Xicheng Tao
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: This paper addresses the challenges of fragmented information, cumbersome processes, and inefficient manual record-keeping in university student quality development management. We present the design and implementation of a mobile-based Smart Campus Student Quality Development Management APP. The system utilizes the uni-app cross-platform framework for frontend development, integrates the Spring Boot backend framework, and employs an SQL Server database, establishing a layered architecture comprising module, basic function, and dependency layers. Through a multi-role permission tagging mechanism (student, activity organizer, reviewer, administrator), the system implements four core functional modules: (1) Activity Operations - enabling activity registration, special credit applications, and proposal submission/approval; (2) Information Center - integrating notification bulletins, automated activity group chats, and centralized message management; (3) Toolbox - providing venue queries (interfaced with academic systems), activity map navigation, and itinerary management; and (4) Personal Center - displaying detailed breakdowns of six quality development credit categories (moral, intellectual, physical, aesthetic, labor, practical) with authentication and feedback portals. The system comprehensively covers the entire quality development management lifecycle. It significantly enhances information synchronization efficiency and data accuracy, reduces administrative overhead, increases student engagement, and advances intelligent campus management.

title: A Low-Supply, Sub-mW 1 GHz Ring Oscillator Achieving < 5% Frequency Variation Across PVT for Reliable On-Chip Clocking
authors: Vellanki Venkata Sundara Raghava Chetan Krishna, Busam Karthikeya, Santosh Yachareni, Saraswatula Sree Rama Krishna Chaithnya, Abhishek Srivastava
publish: 2026 39th International Conference on VLSI Design &amp; 25th International Conference on Embedded Systems (VLSID)
abstract: This paper presents design of low-power, low-area, PVT-invariant 3-stage differential ring oscillator (RO) targeting a frequency of 1 GHz. The design uses a process-trimmed, temperature-compensated robust current reference, designed in TSMC 65 nm CMOS technology, achieving a temperature coefficient as low as 21.67 ppm/° C across temperatures from −40° C to 125° C. A detailed, quantitative simulation focussed design methodology and implemenation is proposed for the design of the current reference and mirroring circuit. The resulting oscillator shows less than 0.5% frequency variation across temperature and a worst-case variation of only 4.78% across PVT. To ensure sub-1V supply operation, only lowthreshold (LVT) MOSFETs are used throughout the design. The proposed design is a low power design and the oscillator consumes approximately 0.36 mW of power at 800 mV supply. This power efficient architecture is well-suited for PVT-robust on-chip clocking in modern low-voltage systems.

title: FPGA-Based 2-Phase Asynchronous Circuit Design for High Performance Computing
authors: Suman Kalyan Porel, Subhadeep Nag, Hemanta Kumar Mondal, Aniruddha Chandra
publish: 2026 39th International Conference on VLSI Design &amp; 25th International Conference on Embedded Systems (VLSID)
abstract: This work explores the design methodology and FPGA-based realization of asynchronous circuits composed of static data-flow handshake components governed by the two-phase bundled-data (BD) protocol. The methodology integrates tutorial-driven exposition with formal architectural analysis, emphasizing a rigorous approach to initialization strategies and the construction of coupled ring structures with arbitrary token distributions. Central to the design flow is the use of Mousetrap-style control templates, enabling localized, transition-driven handshake logic. The paper substantiates its methodology through a comprehensive power-performance-area evaluation, including gate-level synthesis of core handshaking primitives in a 32 nm CMOS technology node. Furthermore, it incorporates peephole-level optimizations, wherein composite components such as merged Join-register or MUX-register structures are synthesized for improved design efficiency. The practicality of the design flow is ultimately validated through the implementation of a Fibonacci sequence generator, showcasing correct functionality and handshake compliance across all components. All modules and the target circuits are verified through full-system deployment on Xilinx Basys 3 FPGA board, confirming robustness and functional correctness of the protocol under real hardware conditions. The average PPA metric of the Mousetrap controller has improved to 38%.

title: XGBoost-Based Approach for Reliable Breast Cancer Detection on the MIAS Dataset
authors: Rekha Baghel, Ela Kumar
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: Breast cancer detection is one of the major concern of the world. Many women dies due to late recognition of the breast cancer. Various researchers proposed many methods to detect the breast cancer but there is a need of the method which give high accurate, minimizes the false positives and false negatives. In this research work XGBoost based model is proposed to detect the breast cancer. MIAS dataset is employed to assess the model's performance Our proposed model achieved 94% accuracy on MIAS dataset. Results indicates that our model outperformed other methods.

title: Personalization at Scale: Leveraging AI for User Centric 0 1 Product Design
authors: Mohana Sudha Karumuri, Thiffany Belda
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Traditional personalization systems require substantial user bases and historical data. We propose a framework for embedding AI driven personalization into zero to one (0 1) product design the earliest stages where product market fit is discovered. Our approach addresses: (1) dynamic onboarding using reinforcement learning, (2) bootstrapping with limited data through few shot learning, and (3) incorporating ethical safeguards from the outset. We present a multi layer architecture integrating contextual bandits, large language models, and human centered design. Experiments on controlled environments and a real world pilot study with 847 users demonstrate that RL based onboarding improves 7 day retention by 18.3% (p < 0.01) and reduces time to first value by 23.7%, while maintaining fairness with disparate impact ratio exceeding 0.9.

title: Improving Intrusion Detection Systems Using Machine Learning for Enhanced Automation and Threat Identification
authors: Nafiz Mahamud, Md. Jamal Uddin
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Innovations in Engineering, Science and Technology for Sustainable Development (ICEST)
abstract: In an era marked by unprecedented digital connectivity, the need for robust network security has never been more critical. Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) serve as a frontline defense against malicious activities within networks and systems, scanning for suspicious behavior in real-time. With the increasing complexity of network traffic, enhancing the accuracy of these systems is paramount to minimizing false alarms and maximizing detection efficacy. This study has explored effective approaches to improve IDS performance through advanced machine learning techniques, specifically Logistic Regression and Random Forest. By leveraging the NSL-KDD knowledge discovery dataset,we have implemented a systematic classification methodology designed to navigate the intricacies of massive network traffic data. Our findings have revealed significant advancements in detectionrates (98%) while reducing erroneous alerts, ultimately contributing to more resilient network security frameworks. We delve into the fusion of machine learning and intrusion detection to fortify the digital landscape against emerging threats.

title: Stories from China: Nondestructive 3D Imaging of Electric Tree Evolution via In Situ Autofluorescence
authors: Zepeng Lv
abstract: Polymers serve as foundational insulating materials critical for the operational safety of power equipment and integrated circuits. However, during service, stresses from multi-physical fields (electrical, thermal, mechanical) can induce the formation of fractal electrical tree damage at composite interfaces. These micro- to nano-scale conductive channels are characterized by trunk diameters ranging from several to tens of micrometers, terminating in submicron tips [1], [2]. Polymer interfaces govern electrical tree evolution through competing mechanisms. On one hand, they act as weak links where electric fields concentrate because of material compatibility mismatches [3]; on the other hand, they can form inhibitory barriers via localized chain conformational adjustments. The propagation of electrical trees triggers a catastrophic cascade, initiating a process that evolves from localized microstructural degradation to systemic failure and, ultimately, insulation breakdown. This behavioral complexity, coupled with the unique physicochemical properties of interfaces and the severity of the resulting damage, presents a core challenge in dielectric failure research. Consequently, the integration of in situ three-dimensional (3D) characterization techniques [4], [5] to investigate the micro-dynamic processes of interfacial electrical trees is critical: it not only advances the science of interfacial insulation, but also provides key support for improving the service reliability of power equipment.

title: Advancing Digital Transformation in UAE Construction: A Policy and Performance Review of Industry 4.0 Technologies
authors: Noor Ahmed Alkhafaji, Mohammad Shamsuzzaman, Emad Mushtaha
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: The adoption of Industry 4.0 technologies, such as Building Information Modeling (BIM), Artificial Intelligence (AI), the Internet of Things (IoT), and Additive Manufacturing, has transformed worldwide construction approaches, enhancing efficiency, sustainability, and decision-making. The UAE has been at the forefront of construction digitalization, demonstrated by initiatives such as the Dubai BIM Mandate and regulatory frameworks like the Dubai Building Code (2021) and the Al Safat Green Building System (2023). Despite these regulatory efforts, gaps exist in the extensive adoption of Industry 4.0 tools throughout the sector. This report thoroughly analyzes the role of Industry 4.0 on the construction sector in the UAE, evaluating the degree of its adoption, regulatory challenges, and possible policy enhancements. The study analyzes international benchmarks, emphasizing exemplary practices from smart cities like Singapore and the United Kingdom. The study employs a policy analytic approach to identify challenges, including financial restrictions, skill shortages, and regulatory inconsistencies, that limit digital adoption. Furthermore, it offers policy recommendations to synchronize Industry <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4. 0}$</tex> technologies with the UAE's sustainability and innovation goals, ensuring the sector's competitiveness and readiness for the future. This paper aims to support the UAE's vision of a smarter and more sustainable construction industry by bridging policy gaps and fostering digital transformation. To strengthen the empirical contribution, a survey with a target sample size of approximately 200 respondents was designed, ensuring statistically reliable adoption metrics to complement the policy analysis.

title: Method for Improving Adhesion Strength of Insulator Surface Coatings Based on Plasma Modification
authors: Ziwen Deng, Yue Pang, Youshuo Liu, Jiaming Cheng, Cheng Pan
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: To address the issue of coating detachment on basin-type insulators used in Gas Insulated Switchgear (GIS) for far-sea offshore wind power applications, a plasma modification technique based on Dielectric Barrier Discharge (DBD) is proposed to enhance the interfacial adhesion strength. This study first pretreated the epoxy resin matrix using plasma technology, followed by the preparation and coating of silicon carbide (SiC)/epoxy composite coatings. Based on this, the influence of treatment time on coating adhesion strength was systematically investigated. Furthermore, through mechanical property testing and physicochemical characterization techniques, the mechanism underlying the enhancement of coating adhesion strength was thoroughly explored. The results indicate that plasma treatment can increase the coating adhesion strength from 5.477 MPa to 15.087 MPa (an improvement of approximately 157%), reaching saturation at 7 minutes. The core mechanism involves the synergistic enhancement of interfacial adhesion strength through three modes introduced by plasma treatment: mechanical interlocking, physical adsorption, and chemical bonding. This research provides key technical support for the engineering application of coatings on insulator surfaces.

title: Blockchain and Ai-Enhanced Dual-Path Framework for Secure and Intelligent Certificate Issuance and Verification
authors: Farhad Alam, Shariq Ahmad, Upinder Kaur, Durgesh Srivastava, Jitendra Kumar, Amit Garg
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: In the global digital era, we encounter ourselves in the necessary of ascertaining the secure issuance and verification of certificates, as these are crucial to checking credibility among institutions and preventing fraud. Traditional Paper-based and centralized digital systems are at High risk of forgery, duplication, and data breaches, emphasizing the requirement for a strong, scalable, tamper-proof framework. Therefore, in this paper, an advanced two-way glide path for certificate management is proposed that desegregates blockchain technology, cryptography, fuzzy logic, and artificial intelligence (AI) to accomplish more secure and round-the-clock issuance, as well as intelligent verification. In this paper, the appendage of issuance creates unique cryptographic hashes, Universally Unique Identifiers (UUIDs), and digital signatures, then cuts QR codes and uses fuzzy signatures to endure small repugnance“s from physical or digital documents. Certificates are stored on a decentralized infrastructure (IPFS) and dropped anchor to blockchain networks for verifiable records. The verification process applied a two-stage methodology that combined blockchain validation with AI analysis, changing the use of Optical Character Recognition (OCR) and deep learning toolsets to verify both metadata integrity and visual authenticity. A fuzzy logic decision layer synthesizes these analyses, relegating certificates as Valid, Valid with Low Confidence, or Invalid. Experimental evaluation evidences the system's superior performance, achieving a fraud detection accuracy of 97.2 %, an average verification latency of 3.8 seconds, perfect recall (zero false negatives), and high discriminative power with ROC AUC of 0.9804 and PR-AUC of 0.9770. The proposed framework provides a scalable, secure, and user-friendly solution applicable across education, corporate, healthcare, government, and recruitment sectors. Future enhancements may include smart contract integration, multi-chain interoperability, and resilience against adversarial attacks, positioning the system as a future-ready platform for global certificate management and verification.

title: SLCC-Based Zonal Power Reciprocal Technology and Engineering Application
authors: Xintong Mao, Jian Ling, Bin Bai, Xianmeng Zhang, Dongbin Lu, Zhenhua Zhu, Zhiwei Wang
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: The multi-Source Line Commutated Converter (SLCC) technology for HVDC transmission integrates a Line Commutated Converter (LCC) with a valve-side directly connected three-phase Static Var & Filter (SVF). This hybrid topology combines voltage-source and current-source characteristics, effectively overcoming limitations of traditional LCC systems, such as high dependency on AC grid voltage strength and insufficient operational flexibility. This paper investigates the SLCC-based zonal power reciprocal technology at the Lingkou receiving station in the Yangzhou-Zhenjiang HVDC Transmission Phase II Project. The topology of SLCC under the zonal power reciprocal mode is presented, followed by the calculation of critical operational parameters. Control strategies and protection configurations specific to this mode are introduced, with comparative analysis against conventional HVDC transmission schemes. Finally, comprehensive verification of SLCC's transient/steady-state performance, fault ride-through capability, and protection system effectiveness is conducted via the RTDS simulation platform.

title: Sustainable IoT-Enabled Healthcare Systems: Architecture, Bibliometric Insights, and Machine Learning Evaluation
authors: Tarandeep Kaur Bhatia, Vinod Kumar Shukla, Daksh Gupta, Arjun Singh, Vishan Kumar Gupta
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: The Internet of Things has emerged as a disruptive technology in the medical sector, providing the possibility of making medical decisions based on the data, conducting complex diagnostics, and monitoring in real time. Bibliometric analysis facilitates the formulation of the paper, where the concept Healthcare IoT application, its involved technology, and existing research trends are analyzed in this paper. The proposed IoT-based healthcare design offers intelligent, safe, energy-efficient healthcare services based on tiered and modular architecture comprised of device, edge, cloud, and provider layers. To achieve a flexible and efficient workflow of the operational process, each of the layers is specially designed to enable it to respond to problems in data gathering, pre-processing, analytics, and clinical decision support. System performance was tested using several machine learning models that include SVM, Random Forest, XGBoost, KNN, ANN, CNN and LSTM. The detection accuracy, precision, and recall metrics were used to evaluate the effectiveness of the models. Experimental findings indicate the effectiveness of the proposed framework in ensuring medical practices are sustainable, healthcare responsive, and able to make accurate decisions. New trends, identification of the gaps between research and the proposals of further directions of IoT-driven healthcare innovation are also discovered during the bibliometric study. The findings point to the opportunities of using IoT together with advanced analytics to provide real-time, patient-centered care and promote the development of intelligent, scalable, and sustainable healthcare. This work provides a solid foundation when it comes to research in the creation of data-driven and energy-efficient healthcare solutions that could contribute to the improvement of the therapeutic outcomes besides overcoming operational and technological challenges in modern medical institutions.

title: Upgrading Svc's Firing Circuitry Improves Performance
authors: Eric Martinez, Bernardo Sainz, Jose Luis Pepe, Jorge Fernandez
publish: 2025 IEEE IAS Pulp and Paper Industry Conference (PPIC)
abstract: A Static VAR Compensator (SVC) regulates voltage stability, improves power factor, and compensates for reactive power in electrical systems. SVC's above the 100 MW range predominantly employs Thyristor Controlled Reactor technology, which has been in use for over 30 years. While thyristor valves remain operational, controls and firing circuitry face lack of spare parts availability, service support and face integration challenges with new technologies. Advances in Hi-Performance Real-Time Controls allows the possibility to generate optimal control solution with tailored interfaces to existing Power devices. This article presents the design, installation, and benefits of the implementation, presenting advantages, efficiency, and versatility versus a complete valve replacement.

title: A General Framework For Deepfake Video Detection
authors: Dema Mohd Al-Thani
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Deepfake technology poses significant challenges to digital trust by generating highly convincing but synthetic multimedia content. This work focuses on advanced deepfake detection methodologies utilising deep convolutional neural networks (CNNs), specifically leveraging the EfficientNet-B6 architecture. The research employs the comprehensive DeepFake Detection Challenge (DFDC) dataset, known for its extensive diversity and scale. A robust preprocessing phase involved strategic frame extraction and precise facial cropping, ensuring optimal data quality, for training the detection model. Evaluation metrics, including Area Under the Curve (AUC) and Log Loss, were systematically applied to assess model performance. The results indicated strong performance of the EfficientNet-B6 model in accurately distinguishing manipulated videos from authentic content within the DFDC dataset. This project highlights the critical need for continually evolving deepfake detection algorithms to effectively respond to increasingly sophisticated generation techniques, thereby contributing to the broader initiative of combating digital misinformation.

title: Residential Demand and Efficiency in Saudi Arabia's Net Zero Journey
authors: Mohammad Aldubyan
publish: 2025 Global Conference on Sustainable Energy and Net-Zero Emissions (SENZE)
abstract: With Saudi Arabia's ambitious pledge to achieve net zero emissions by 2060, reducing domestic electricity consumption-and consequently lowering greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions-becomes a critical priority. Given that the residential sector is the largest consumer of electricity in the Kingdom, understanding how its demand will evolve over the long term is essential for informing effective policy design. This paper pursues two main objectives: first, to forecast residential electricity demand through 2060; and second, to evaluate the potential impact of energy efficiency (EE) improvements in moderating this demand. We employ an Autoregressive Integrated Moving Average (ARIMA) model based on historical residential electricity consumption data to project future trends. Building on this baseline forecast, we assess the mitigation potential of energy efficiency using a bottom-up building stock model customized for Saudi Arabia. The EE assessment considers both new residential buildings and retrofits of the existing housing stock. Our results indicate that electricity demand will continue its upward trajectory over the next four decades. However, enhancing EE standards-particularly for building insulation, air conditioning, lighting, and major household appliances-can reduce cumulative electricity consumption by 18% over the forecast period and cut demand in 2060 by 30%. These measures would also help avoid approximately 690 million tonnes of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CO}_{2}$</tex> emissions over the coming decades, including 39 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$MtCO_{2}$</tex> in 2060 alone.

title: Study on Magnetic Core Loss Modeling Based on Temperature-Corrected Steinmetz Equation
authors: Yao Xiao, Shengwen Shu, Guobin Wang, Xiaolin Xu, Kang Wang, Bing Liu
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: To address the problem that the original Steinmetz equation does not account for temperature factors and lacks accuracy in predicting core loss under varying temperature conditions, this paper conducts research on core loss modeling based on a temperature-corrected Steinmetz equation. Firstly, the temperature sensitivity deficiencies of the original Steinmetz equation are analyzed, highlighting its deviation from measured core losses at different temperatures. Then, based on the potential correlation between core loss and temperature, three types of temperature-corrected Steinmetz equations—linear, quadratic, and exponential power—are constructed, and their model parameters are fitted using a genetic algorithm. Finally, the model performance is evaluated using the coefficient of determination and mean squared error. The results indicate that the exponential power temperature-corrected equation demonstrates significantly better goodness-of-fit than the original equation, effectively reducing the impact of temperature variations on core loss prediction; the linear temperature-corrected equation also shows good accuracy, whereas the quadratic temperature-corrected equation performs poorly. The temperature-corrected models developed in this study resolve the temperature adaptability issue of the original Steinmetz equation and provide reliable support for accurate core loss prediction in high-frequency power converters.

title: A Bi-Level Stochastic Framework with Hybrid PSO-GWO for VPP Co-Optimization of Economic and Grid Support Objectives
authors: Anees Ahmad, Usama Aslam, Vikram Kumar, Touseef Ahmed, Muhammad Ahsan Niazi, Muhammad Shahzad Aziz
publish: 2025 4th Asia Power and Electrical Technology Conference (APET)
abstract: The integration of Distributed Energy Resources (DERs) into power systems is facilitated by Virtual Power Plants (VPPs). However, managing a VPP is complex due to stochastic generation and the conflict between economic and technical goals. This paper proposes a novel bi-level stochastic optimization framework for VPP management. Its primary contributions are: 1) a Hybrid Particle Swarm Optimization-Grey Wolf Optimizer (PSO-GWO) that synergistically enhances solution quality and avoids local optima, and 2) a dynamic, grid-aware fitness function allowing the VPP to autonomously shift priority between profit and grid support based on real-time conditions. The model incorporates high-fidelity components, including Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) with battery degradation. The upper level minimizes cost, while the lower level enforces security via power flow on a modified IEEE 14-bus system. Simulations demonstrate the framework's superiority, showing a 22% higher mean profit and significantly reduced financial risk over deterministic methods. The VPP also improves grid stability, enhancing voltage profiles and reducing critical line congestion by up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 3. 6 \%}$</tex>. A Pareto front analysis quantifies the cost-stability trade-off, offering a practical tool for operators. The results validate the framework as a robust, intelligent solution for high-performance VPP operation.

title: Field-Based Image Acquisition and Deep Learning Framework for Early-Stage Detection of Rice Leaf Folder (Cnaphalocrocis Medinalis) Integrating Environmental Metadata
authors: Jeyagopal Janarththanan, Gunasingham Mikunthan, Sidath Ravindra Liyanage, Tharaga Sharmilan
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Innovations in Engineering, Science and Technology for Sustainable Development (ICEST)
abstract: Timely and accurate detection of rice leaf folder (Cnaphalocrocis medinalis) infestations is critical for minimizing crop damage in rice cultivation, especially under real-world field conditions. This study presents a scalable framework for early pest detection using field-based image acquisition and deep learning model selection. High-resolution RGB images of healthy and infested rice leaves were collected from Sri Lankan paddy fields across two cropping seasons, along with GPStagged environmental metadata retrieved via the NASA POWER API. The resulting dataset of 805 manually annotated images was used to evaluate multiple deep learning architectures. Two models, YOLOv8 and DenseNet201, were selected for final testing. DenseNet201 achieved the highest classification accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(93.87 \%)$</tex>, while YOLOv8 offered rapid inference <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(85-100 ~\text{ms})$</tex>, supporting real-time deployment on mobile devices. Evaluation on a held-out test set confirmed strong sensitivity and specificity, with low false prediction rates. The integration of consumer-grade imaging and AI-driven analytics highlights the framework's potential for practical, onfield use in sustainable, precision pest management systems.

title: Analysis of Brain Tumor Segmentation Using Deep Learning and Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Ankita Rani, Deepankar Bharadwaj
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: MRI diagnosis and the regular categorization of numerous types of brain tumors are crucial for beforehand detection and effective clinical treatment. This paper presents the technique of machine learning and deep learning which is based on approaches to improve the general accuracy and reliability of detecting brain tumors. A dataset containing 255 MRI images were utilized, and the advanced preprocessing techniques involved adaptive enhancement of image contrast, noise reduction, and segmentation using the Fuzzy C-means algorithm to prepare the data. The feature extracted methods involved the enrichment of Gray-Level Co-occurrence Matrix in a dataset. The developed combination Deep Neural Support Vector-Machines model detection had an accuracy of 97.79 percentage for EDN-SVM and outperformed previous techniques. Such a robust nature of this model was again validated on various metrics with regard to sensitivity, specificity, Jaccard Coefficient, and demonstrated great clinical application possibilities in supporting the radiologist to spot correct and time-efficient diagnostic process.

title: Deep Learning-Based Credit Risk Prediction Using Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: Said Salloum, Khalaf Tahat, Ahmed Mansoori, Chaker Mhamdi, Baghdad Alshanaq, Dina Tahat
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Communication, Networking and Services (ICCNS)
abstract: Credit scoring has always been conceived as an important element to financial risk evaluation enabling institutions to make informed lending decisions. Conventional credit scoring employs historical financial information and rule-based models that tend not to capture non-linear trends in consumer behavior. Machine learning and other models can improve risk prediction through an empirical approach. In this paper, we examine the performance of a CNN derived approach for credit behaviour prediction using the Kaggle Credit Card Behaviour Score dataset. The ROC AUC score of the model is 1.00, indicating a very good classification. Results suggest that CNNs provide an opportunity to enhance credit risk prediction by discovering novel financial patterns. Challenges of overfitting and class imbalance need further tuning for implementation in financial decision-making.

title: Boosting Saliency Perception with Self-Prompting in Abstractive Summarization
authors: Fengyu Lu, Haifeng Jing, Jiaxin Duan
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: Abstractive summarization models learned with maximum likelihood estimation (MLE) tend to generate bland summaries that mismatch human concerns. This is because MLE only minimizes the token-level discrepancies between the model output and the reference summary, failing to indicate a model that captures salient input information. To address this problem, we propose a novel self-prompting summarization paradigm (SS), which requires a model to generate salient document spans (i.e., prompt) first and then a succeeding abstractive summary. Additionally, to ensure the model-generated prompt is always conducive for generating a desirable summary, we enumerate prompt-summary combinations across varying matching degrees and calibrate the SS model with the corresponding handcrafted artificial samples. Extensive experiments are conducted on four well-known datasets to test our method. Results reveal that the calibrated SS model can generate summaries that are more compatible with human interest. It also exhibits new state-of-the-art performance evaluated by ROUGE scores in moderate abstractive scenarios alongside improved saliency and faithfulness. We further deeply discuss the properties of our methods through empirical analyses.

title: Ethical Risks and Preventive Strategies for AI Integration in K-12 Education
authors: Shuang jiao, Chuyue Feng, Ning Li, Yucen Cui, Jing Gao, Xiaolong Ding
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: This study systematically explores the ethical risks associated with the integration of artificial intelligence (AI) in K-12 educational contexts, emphasizing issues such as algorithmic bias, privacy, autonomy, and educational inequity. By synthesizing relevant literature and examining practical cases, we propose a structured ethical risk prevention strategy. This strategy encompasses policy recommendations, targeted professional development for educators, and student-focused AI literacy programs. The research highlights a balanced framework aiming to ensure ethical, equitable, and effective deployment of AI in educational environments.

title: Skin Cancer Classification Via Shifted Window Attention: A Swin Transformer Approach
authors: Maher Alrahhal, Nabeel Al Yateem, Talal Bonny, Mawieh Hamad, Mohammad Ahmad Qasim Al-Shabi, Abir Jaafar Hussain
publish: 2025 Advances in Science and Engineering Technology International Conferences (ASET)
abstract: Skin cancer is among the most prevalent and potentially life-threatening malignancies worldwide, making early and accurate diagnosis essential for effective treatment. This study introduces SwinSkinNet, a Swin Transformer-based deep learning framework designed for automated classification of dermoscopic skin lesion images into seven clinically significant categories. By leveraging a hierarchical vision transformer architecture with shifted window-based self-attention, SwinSkinNet effectively captures both local texture and global structural features. The model was evaluated on a curated subset of the ISIC dataset comprising 1,990 annotated images spanning both malignant and benign lesion types. Experimental results demonstrate the model's robustness, achieving an average accuracy of 98.0%, F1-score of 91.88%, and area under the curve (AUC) of 0.9586. High recall for malignant lesions such as melanoma and squamous cell carcinoma, along with perfect precision for benign classes like pigmented keratosis, underscores the model's clinical applicability. SwinSkinNet offers a scalable and efficient solution for dermatological decision support systems, mobile diagnostics, and teledermatology platforms, particularly beneficial in resource-constrained settings.

title: Accelerating Large Language Models' Training and Fine-Tuning Using GPU Task Prediction Models
authors: Khondker Shajadul Hasan, Likhitha Sadavala, Alfredo J. Perez-Davila, Ahmed Abukmail
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Communication, Networking and Services (ICCNS)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are powerful AI systems utilized in various applications such as chatbots and translation tools. However, training these models is resource-intensive, demanding significant computational power, particularly from GPUs. Current training methods encounter inefficiencies, resulting in extended training times and heightened resource consumption. This research identifies key overheads in existing LLM training techniques and introduces a novel GPU task execution performance prediction model. By incorporating this model into LLM training workflows, the study aims to decrease training time while ensuring model quality. The integration of the GPU task prediction model led to a considerable enhancement in computational efficiency, reducing training time by roughly 3.34 times compared to traditional methods. This improvement in efficiency underscores the potential of predictive performance modeling in optimizing resource utilization and accelerating the fine-tuning process of LLMs without compromising their quality.

title: Construction of a Multimodal Medicinal Plant Corpus for Tibetan Medicine Knowledge Management
authors: Pema Tseden, Lhamo Kyap, Yong Tso, Nyima Tashi
publish: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Information Processing (AICSIP)
abstract: Tibetan medicine, a key component of traditional Chinese medicine, faces significant challenges in digitization and structured knowledge organization due to its reliance on unstructured classical texts. To address this issue, this study constructs a standardized multimodal corpus of Tibetan medicinal plants, integrating textual, taxonomic, and morphological image data. Based on systematic analysis of classical works such as Crystal Materia Medica , The Stainless Crystal Mirror , and Chinese Materia Medica: Tibetan Medicine Volume , a structured extraction framework was developed. Nomenclature inconsistencies were resolved through hierarchical standardization and computational techniques (e.g., Levenshtein distance). The resulting corpus includes 224 commonly used species, each annotated with standardized Tibetan, Latin, and Chinese names, taxonomic classifications, ecological attributes, and 2240 high-resolution images. This corpus provides a valuable digital foundation for accurate identification, intelligent analysis, and cross-disciplinary research, supporting the preservation, modernization, and international communication of Tibetan medical knowledge.

title: A Review of Non-Invasive Anemia Detection Using Image Processing and Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Anchal Chaudhary, Mamta Bansal, Mukesh Rawat
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Advanced Computing and Emerging Technologies (ACET)
abstract: Anemia, in particular, iron deficiency anemia, is a global health issue that has remained over the years, and it is the most common among women of childbearing age. At present, physicians rely on blood tests to confirm the condition, but such tests are not always readily available and are costly in less developed areas. This research introduces a novel method for diagnosing anemia that does not involve blood withdrawal but rather a smartphone application named Aarogya Shakti.The application records a video of the wrist and then analyses the video for the features that could be the indicators of the low hemoglobin level that is the main component of red blood cells. It employs traditional machine learning techniques such as Random Forest and Support Vector Machine (SVM) along with a deep learning model named Iron Deficiency NN constructed using PyTorch to be able to quantify the risk of anemia with high accuracy. As for the time being, the model incorporates only time-dependent features, however, it is already set up to utilize CNNs later on for more precise outcomes. The app delivers an instant result of the anemia risk to the user and also provides health tips. The device, which is simple to operate and inexpensive, could potentially open up a new path for people living in remote areas without regular medical care to receive proper health care. Through AI and contactless imaging, this study is a p Step forward in making anemia screening more accessible and, consequently, it can contribute to women's health improvement globally.

title: Hybrid Additive Manufacturing for Embedded Electronics: A Process Integration of FFF and Direct Circuit Printing
authors: Guido Di Bella, Luigi Calabrese, Laura Arruzzoli, Mariangela Latino, Giovanni Gugliandolo, Nicola Donato
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Green Technologies, Renewable Energy and Ecological Sustainability (MetroGREENST)
abstract: The integration of structural and functional materials through additive manufacturing opens new perspectives for the development of smart components with embedded electronics. In this study, a novel multi-material and multi-process strategy is proposed to embed conductive circuits directly into 3D-printed PLA substrates. The workflow combines fused filament fabrication (FFF) with direct-write circuit printing. A conductive trace integrated within the PLA structure was fabricated and characterized, showing a resistance of (103.5 ± 0.1) Ω. The process also includes overprinting an additional PLA layer to encapsulate the circuit, enabling the creation of monolithic parts with embedded, protected conductive paths. This technique demonstrates the potential of integrating mechanical and electrical functionalities within a single, additively manufactured part.

title: Fast Tensor Convolver Multiplier for Exact Integer Convolution and Multiplication
authors: Mehmet Emre Ciftcibasi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: This paper introduces the Tensor Convolver Multiplier (TCM), a unified exact algorithm for integer convolution and multiplication. In contrast to FFT- or NTT-based approaches, TCM operates entirely in the integer domain and does not require zero-padding, wraparound correction, or transformation to an auxiliary numerical domain.The method is based on tensor products and adaptive dimensional folding, inspired by geometric right-hand-rule constructions. Multiplication corresponds to a receiver-like operation that collapses multi-dimensional inputs into a scalar through tensor folding and delayed carry propagation, while convolution corresponds to a transmitter-like operation that expands a scalar source into structured sequences via direction-aware folding.The main contributions are:•Exactness: TCM performs integer convolution and multiplication entirely in Z, without padding, modular arithmetic, or floating-point roundoff.•Total work: For two N-digit operands, the arithmetic work is W(N) = Θ(N<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>), consisting of outer-product partial products and carry-free diagonal accumulation, matching the schoolbook bound while preserving exactness.•Critical path (ideal parallelism): With sufficient parallel resources, latency is dominated by tree-style diagonal reductions and folding, yielding span C(N) = O(logN). For batched or product-tree folding over M operands using an adaptive a-ary folding primitive, the span becomes C(M,N) = O(logN+log<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">a</inf> M), highlighting reduced reduction depth compared to binary accumulation.The geometric-arithmetic duality that bridges algebraic exactness and physical interpretability, positions TCM as a hardware primitive for all systems that will benefit from fast, exact results.

title: Fish Species Classification and Skin Disease Detection in Underwater Images Using YOLO and CNN
authors: Ayman Ghadouna, Mohamed Adam Sterheltou, Mohamed Hadadi, Yousra Chtouki
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: This paper presents a deep learning framework for fish species classification, image classification, and skin disease type classification in underwater images. Our method integrates the object detection capabilities of YOLO with a CNN for classification. Two image datasets Fish Species Image Data and Freshwater Fish Disease Aquaculture in South Asia are used for training. Experimental results report a mean Average Precision (mAP) of 78% for fish detection and an overall classification accuracy of 85% for fish species and specific skin disease types. The CNN distinguishes among multiple disease categories (e.g., Aeromoniasis, bacterial gill disease, bacterial red disease, fungal diseases, parasitic diseases, and White tail disease), and the error rate considers misclassification of disease types.

title: Optimal Placement and Sizing of FACTS Devices in Radial Distribution Systems Using Snooker-Based Optimization Algorithm
authors: Khawlah Yahya Rebat
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Radial Distribution Systems (RDS) often suffer from voltage drops, high power losses, and instability due to long feeders and varying load conditions. To address these challenges, this study investigates the use of Flexible AC Transmission Systems (FACTS), specifically the Thyristor Controlled Series Compensator (TCSC) and Static Var Compensator (SVC), to enhance voltage stability and minimize losses. Different deployment scenarios, including simultaneous placement and positional interchanges, were analyzed on the IEEE 33-bus test system. The Snooker-Based Optimization Algorithm (SBOA) was employed to determine optimal device placement and sizing, offering superior convergence speed and solution accuracy compared to conventional methods. Load flow analysis was performed using the Primitive Impedance-Based Distribution Load Flow (PIDLF) method, ensuring computational efficiency and numerical stability. Additionally, both Normal and ZIP load models were considered to reflect realistic load behaviors. The results demonstrate that optimized integration of FACTS devices significantly improves the performance and reliability of radial distribution networks.

title: Scheduling Linear Workflows Along with Critical Periodic Jobs with Slack Time in a Distributed System
authors: Helen D. Karatza
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Communication, Networking and Services (ICCNS)
abstract: Distributed computing systems are designed to meet the quality of service (QoS) requirements of diverse applications. Achieving this goal necessitates the deployment of efficient task scheduling strategies that ensure the correct and timely execution of heterogeneous workloads. This study focuses on scheduling techniques for linear workflow (LW) applications in the presence of higher-priority, soft real-time periodic tasks. While these real-time tasks typically preempt LW execution, they can, under certain conditions, tolerate bounded slack time without violating their temporal constraints. This flexibility enables the scheduler to reduce the disruption to LW execution. We propose a scheduling approach that effectively manages soft real-time critical periodic tasks while minimizing the performance degradation of LW applications. Through simulation-based evaluation, we analyze the impact of slack time-conditioned on the execution progress of LW tasks-on overall system performance across multiple scenarios.

title: Implementation of Ensemble-Sine Cosine Algorithm in Prediction of VEGFR-2 Inhibitors as Anti-Cancer Agents
authors: Cut Nabilah Nuur Azizah, Angel Metanosa Afinda, Isman Kurniawan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computer and Applications (ICCA)
abstract: Cancer remains a leading cause of global mortality, with more than 20 million new cases reported in 2022, making VEGFR-2 inhibition serving as a pivotal therapeutic strategy. While machine learning aids inhibitor discovery, high-dimensional molecular datasets often hinder predictive performance due to noise and sparsity. This study proposes a robust framework integrating ensemble learning with the Sine Cosine Algorithm (SCA) for optimized feature selection. Utilizing a dataset from Gupta and his colleagues research, containing 118 molecules with 1,444 descriptors, the methodology employs ADASYN oversampling within a stratified cross-validation scheme to mitigate class imbalance in the dataset. The proposed SCA-Ensemble approach significantly outperformed baseline models, achieving an average increase from 57% to 79% in F1-score and an average increase from 64% to 81% in ROC-AUC. Notably, the optimized Random Forest model demonstrated the highest effectiveness, achieving a 56% improvement in performance, with an F1-score of 0.86. These findings highlight the framework's potential in identifying critical physicochemical biomarkers for accelerating anti-cancer drug design.

title: Real-Time AI Transformer-Based Contactless Fatigue Detection for Resistance Training
authors: Jinhui Xu, Zijia Qu, Xuben Chang, Colin Pak Yu Chan, Yanchen Liu, Ziqi Li, Xiaoyang Zou, King Wai Chiu Lai
publish: 2025 IEEE 18th International Conference on Nano/Molecular Medicine &amp; Engineering (NANOMED)
abstract: Fatigue monitoring in resistance training is crucial for optimizing performance while preventing injury, yet traditional contact-based methods limit athlete mobility and real-time feedback. This paper presents a contactless fatigue detection system that combines depth camera technology with a multi-task transformer architecture for real-time monitoring during resistance exercises. Our approach captures joint angle data using depth sensors and employs an adaptive sliding window technique that predicts individual cycle durations to enable subject-specific monitoring. The transformer-based architecture with hard parameter sharing processes temporal sequences of joint kinematics through spatiotemporal attention mechanisms, simultaneously performing three synergistic tasks: cycle duration prediction, three-class fatigue state classification, and binary intervention warning with advance notice. Experimental validation on 14 subjects performing squat exercises demonstrates that our system achieves 81.1% accuracy in fatigue state classification and 84.7% accuracy in predictive intervention warning with 2.67-second lead time. The adaptive window mechanism accurately estimates cycle durations with mean absolute error of 0.13 seconds and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}=0.95$</tex>, enabling robust cross-subject generalization. This contactless method enables dynamic training load adjustment while preserving natural movement patterns, representing a practical advancement toward fatigue assessment in sports and rehabilitation applications.
